M&K
Hotel Experience Auditors · Europe

Your guests notice
what your team
no longer sees.

One GM moved from 8.1 to 8.6 on Booking.com in three months. Another rewrote their service recovery the same week the report arrived. Anonymous end-to-end audits — 100-point scored report and a strategic debrief delivered by someone who stayed as an ordinary guest.

Get a Proposal See how it works
100
Point framework
12
Touchpoints
7
Years experience
Scroll
Precision Discretion Excellence Anonymous audit Boutique hotels Independent properties Across Europe Precision Discretion Excellence Anonymous audit Boutique hotels Independent properties Across Europe
“I expected a checklist. I received a strategic document.” — GM, Design hotel · Lisbon
The Audit

One stay.
Every detail.

A Marque & Key auditor books your hotel as an ordinary paying guest — no announcement, no special treatment. We evaluate every touchpoint a real guest encounters, from the first click on your booking page to the farewell at checkout.

Base audit fee
€1,490
+ reimbursed stay costs · EUR · reverse charge VAT
Included in every audit
12-touchpoint anonymous audit visit
100-point scored PDF report within 7–10 business days
GM debrief via video call
Strategic recommendations per category
Audit categoryPointsWeight
Reservation & Pre-Arrival20
Arrival & Check-In20
Room & Physical Spaces20
Food & Beverage15
Service & Guest Interactions20
Check-Out & Post-Stay5
Total score100 pts

alt="Hotel reception — art deco lobby" width="1800" height="1088">
12
Touchpoints
100
Point Framework
7–10
Days to Report

“Guests form their opinion
in the first ninety seconds.”

How it works

Five phases.
Zero compromises.

The process is designed so that your team performs at their natural, everyday level — not a rehearsed version of it. That is the only way the findings are worth acting on.

01
Contract & deposit

Agreement signed, 50% deposit received. Audit window set — the GM knows an audit is coming, not when.

02
Pre-arrival tests

Online booking UX, telephone reservation, and email enquiry evaluated before any physical visit.

03
Anonymous visit

Auditor stays as an ordinary paying guest. All 12 touchpoints assessed in real time. Identity never disclosed.

04
Report delivery

Scored PDF report delivered within 7–10 business days. Balance invoice issued simultaneously.

05
GM debrief

Video session with the General Manager. Findings presented, questions answered, priorities set.

The auditor's identity is never disclosed — not during, not after.

Staff will not know an audit is taking place. The exact date, room booking, and auditor alias remain confidential at all times. This is non-negotiable — and it is what makes the findings credible.

Food & Beverage

Every table tells a story.
We read all of them.

What GMs say

Real results.
From real GMs.

79
Audit
score

"We thought our check-in was our strongest touchpoint. The report showed it ranked last. Three months later our Booking.com score moved from 8.1 to 8.6."

General Manager
Boutique hotel · 38 rooms · Southern France
84
Audit
score

"The complaint scenario test was uncomfortable to read. It was also the most valuable page in the entire report. We rewrote our service recovery procedure the same week."

Owner & General Manager
Independent hotel · 52 rooms · Costa del Sol
91
Audit
score

"I expected a checklist. I received a strategic document. The debrief alone was worth the fee — a focused, evidence-based conversation about our entire operation."

General Manager
Design hotel · 29 rooms · Lisbon
For whom

Built for hotels
that care about
the details.

Strong physical product. Inconsistent service delivery — and you can see it in the reviews. Guests praise the rooms, flag the staff. You suspect the gap is real but you have no objective evidence. That is exactly the profile Marque & Key was built for.

Boutique Independents

Independent properties where the guest experience is a primary differentiator and every review matters.

Typical trigger: Review score plateau
SPA & Wellness Resorts

Properties where the wellness journey extends beyond the treatment room — arrival energy, therapist interactions, post-treatment care.

Typical trigger: Spa revenue underperforming
Fine Dining Hotels

Hotels where F&B is a destination in itself — where service culture, menu knowledge, and table experience define the reputation.

Typical trigger: Inconsistent F&B feedback
New Openings

Hotels in the first 12 months of operation — before bad habits are embedded and while standards can still be set correctly.

Typical trigger: Pre-launch or first season

The properties we audit are chosen by guests
for moments they remember for years.

7
Years in hospitality
About

Not a consulting firm.
A precision instrument.

Marque & Key was built by someone who spent seven years inside the industry — long enough to understand what genuine luxury service feels like, and why it so often falls apart the moment management stops watching.

The audit is not a checklist handed to a hired mystery shopper. Every evaluation is conducted with the same critical eye and professional standard. The methodology is consistent, the criteria are exacting, and the report says what it found — not what the client hopes to hear.

IPrecision100 scored criteria across 6 categories
IIDiscretionAuditor identity never disclosed
IIIExcellenceThe standard we hold ourselves to
Common questions

Everything you need
to decide.

No. Only the General Manager is informed — and only that an audit will happen within a defined window, not the date, time, or identity of the auditor. Your team performs exactly as they would for any ordinary guest. That is the only way the findings are credible.
A lower score is more valuable, not less. It means the audit identified real gaps before your guests did — or before they wrote about them publicly. The report tells you exactly where the issues are and what to do about them. Hotels that score 60–75 typically see the most significant operational improvement after acting on the findings.
Once the agreement is signed and the deposit received, we agree on an audit window of 2–8 weeks. The anonymous visit happens within that window. The scored PDF report is delivered within 7–10 business days of the visit. The GM debrief is scheduled within two weeks of the report.
Completely. Your score, findings, and all report content are covered by a mutual NDA embedded in the agreement. Marque & Key will never publish, reference, or share your results — not even anonymously — without your written consent. The report is yours alone.
Yes — especially so. High-performing hotels use the audit to confirm what is working, identify the 2–3 touchpoints holding them back from exceptional, and produce an internal benchmark to measure against. Several clients have used their score in staff development programmes and ownership reporting.
The auditor stays for two nights as an ordinary paying guest. This is the minimum required to evaluate all 12 touchpoints properly — including the pre-arrival sequence, arrival and check-in, in-room experience, at least one F&B interaction, a complaint scenario, and check-out with post-stay follow-up. A single night would not allow a complete and credible evaluation. The cost of both nights is reimbursed by the hotel on the final invoice.

Ready to find out where your guest experience actually stands?

Privacy Policy Terms of Service
Privacy Policy

Last updated: June 2025

1. Data Controller

The data controller is Marque & Key sp. z o.o. (in registration), operating under the brand Marque & Key, reachable at hello@marqueandkey.com.

2. What data we collect

When you submit the contact form, we collect: hotel name, your role, email address, country, and any voluntary message content.

3. Purpose and legal basis

Your data is processed solely to respond to your enquiry. The legal basis is your consent (Article 6(1)(a) GDPR). You may withdraw consent at any time by emailing hello@marqueandkey.com.

4. How your data is stored

Submissions are processed via Netlify Forms (netlify.com), which forwards them to our email inbox. We do not maintain any additional database of enquiry data.

5. Data retention

Contact data is retained for the duration of any resulting engagement. If no contract is concluded, it is deleted within 12 months of initial contact.

6. Third-party sharing

We do not sell or share your data for marketing. Data is not transferred outside the European Economic Area.

7. Your GDPR rights

You have the right to access, rectify, or erase your data, and to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority. Contact: hello@marqueandkey.com.

8. Cookies

This website uses no tracking cookies or analytics scripts. Google Fonts are loaded from Google's CDN for typography purposes only.

Terms of Service

Last updated: June 2025

1. About this website

This website (marqueandkey.com) is operated by Marque & Key (trading name of Marque & Key sp. z o.o., in registration), a hotel guest experience auditing company. By accessing this website you agree to these Terms.

2. Services described

The services described here are provided under a separate B2B Audit Agreement. These Terms govern website use only. Commercial terms are set out in the signed Audit Agreement for each engagement.

3. Accuracy of information

We make reasonable efforts to keep information accurate. Pricing and scope details are indicative; binding terms are those in the signed Audit Agreement.

4. Intellectual property

All content — text, design, scoring framework, brand identity — is the intellectual property of Marque & Key. Reproduction without written consent is prohibited.

5. Contact form

Submitting the form constitutes an enquiry only and does not create a contractual relationship. We will respond to genuine enquiries within 24 hours.

6. Limitation of liability

Marque & Key shall not be liable for any indirect or consequential loss arising from use of or reliance on information on this website.

7. Governing law

These Terms are governed by Polish law. Disputes shall be subject to the jurisdiction of the competent courts of Poland.

8. Contact

hello@marqueandkey.com
